column1: # History: through M13 this was a single 2 MB `factory` slot with a
# canonical ota_0 + ota_1 + otadata trio. `storage` shrank from ~6 MB
# to ~4 MB — still generous headroom for the sprite cache (which sits
# well under 1 MB resident) and future event log.
# With no `factory` partition the bootloader follows `otadata` to
# choose between ota_0 and ota_1. First USB flash writes to ota_0
# and the bootloader picks it because otadata is blank (== fall back
# to first valid ota_X). Every subsequent OTA update writes to the
# *other* slot and atomically flips otadata on reboot.
# IMPORTANT: this layout is binary-incompatible with the old
# factory-only one. The first install of an OTA-capable image MUST
# come over USB; we can't stage a new bootloader + new partition
# table from an old image's runtime. After that one reflash
# further updates can go OTA. The reflash wipes the LittleFS
# cached sprites are re-fetched on first boot of the OTA image.
# Application code mounts the LittleFS partition by label `storage`

column2: so sprite_cache + future event log code
